heading: Demonstration and training programs
label: 773 Demonstration and training programs

children.heading: Demonstration projects to increase client choice
children.label: a Demonstration projects to increase client choice

children.heading: Grants
children.label: 1 Grants
children.text: The Commissioner may make grants to States and public or nonprofit agencies and organizations to pay all or part of the costs of projects to demonstrate ways to increase client choice in the rehabilitation process, including the selection of providers of vocational rehabilitation services.

children.label: A
children.text: for activities that are directly related to planning, operating, and evaluating the demonstration projects; and

children.label: B
children.text: to supplement, and not supplant, funds made available from Federal and non-Federal sources for such projects.

children.label: i
children.text: how the entity intends to promote increased client choice in the rehabilitation process, including a description, if appropriate, of how an applicant will determine the cost of any service or product offered to an eligible client;

children.label: ii
children.text: how the entity intends to ensure that any vocational rehabilitation service or related service is provided by a qualified provider who is accredited or meets such other quality assurance and cost-control criteria as the State may establish; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the outreach activities to be conducted by the applicant to obtain eligible clients; and

children.label: i
children.text: a statement of the vocational rehabilitation goals to be achieved;

children.label: ii
children.text: a statement of the specific vocational rehabilitation services to be provided, the projected dates for their initiation, and the anticipated duration of each such service; and

children.label: iii
children.text: objective criteria, an evaluation procedure, and a schedule, for determining whether such goals are being achieved.

children.label: A
children.text: the diversity of strategies used to increase client choice, including selection among qualified service providers;

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic distribution of projects; and

children.label: C
children.text: the diversity of clients to be served.

children.heading: Records
children.label: 5 Records
children.text: Entities that receive grants under paragraph (1) shall maintain such records as the Commissioner may require and comply with any request from the Commissioner for such records.

children.heading: Direct services
children.label: 6 Direct services
children.text: At least 80 percent of the funds awarded for any project under this subsection shall be used for direct services, as specifically chosen by eligible clients.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 7 Evaluation
children.text: The Commissioner may conduct an evaluation of the demonstration projects with respect to the services provided, clients served, client outcomes obtained, implementation issues addressed, the cost-effectiveness of the project, and the effects of increased choice on clients and service providers. The Commissioner may reserve funds for the evaluation for a fiscal year from the amounts appropriated to carry out projects under this section for the fiscal year.

children.heading: Direct services
children.label: A Direct services
children.text: The term “direct services” means vocational rehabilitation services, as described in .section 723(a) of this title

children.heading: Eligible client
children.label: B Eligible client
children.text: The term “eligible client” means an individual with a disability, as defined in , who is not currently receiving services under an individualized plan for employment established through a designated State unit.section 705(20)(A) of this title

children.heading: Grants; contracts
children.label: 1 Grants; contracts
children.text: The Commissioner, subject to the provisions of , may provide grants to, or enter into contracts with, eligible entities to pay all or part of the cost of programs that expand and improve the provision of rehabilitation and other services authorized under this chapter or that further the purposes of the chapter, including related research and evaluation activities.section 776 of this title

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: A Eligible entities
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant, or enter into a contract, under paragraph (1), an entity shall be a State vocational rehabilitation agency, community rehabilitation program, Indian tribe or tribal organization, or other public or nonprofit agency or organization, or as the Commissioner determines appropriate, a for-profit organization. The Commissioner may limit competitions to one or more types of organizations described in this subparagraph.

children.heading: Terms and conditions
children.label: B Terms and conditions
children.text: A grant or contract under paragraph (1) shall contain such terms and conditions as the Commissioner may require.

children.label: A
children.text: is based on current research findings, which may include research conducted by the National Institute on Disability, Independent Living, and Rehabilitation Research, the National Institutes of Health, and other public or private organizations; and

children.label: B
children.text: is of national significance.

children.label: A
children.text: special projects and demonstrations of service delivery;

children.label: B
children.text: model demonstration projects;

children.label: C
children.text: technical assistance projects;

children.label: D
children.text: systems change projects;

children.label: E
children.text: special studies and evaluations; and

children.label: F
children.text: dissemination and utilization activities.

children.label: i
children.text: initiatives focused on improving transition from education, including postsecondary education, to employment, particularly in competitive integrated employment, for youth who are individuals with significant disabilities;

children.label: ii
children.text: supported employment, including community-based supported employment programs to meet the needs of individuals with the most significant disabilities or to provide technical assistance to States and community organizations to improve and expand the provision of supported employment services; and

children.label: iii
children.text: increasing competitive integrated employment for individuals with significant disabilities.

children.label: i
children.text: Age ranges.

children.label: ii
children.text: Types of disabilities.

children.label: iii
children.text: Types of services.

children.label: iv
children.text: Models of service delivery.

children.label: v
children.text: Stage of the rehabilitation process.

children.label: vi
children.text: The needs of underserved populations, unserved and underserved areas, individuals with significant disabilities, low-incidence disability population or individuals residing in federally designated empowerment zones and enterprise communities.

children.label: vii
children.text: Expansion of employment opportunities for individuals with disabilities.

children.label: viii
children.text: Systems change projects to promote meaningful access of individuals with disabilities to employment-related services under subtitle B of title I of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act [ et seq.] and under other Federal laws.29 U.S.C. 3151

children.label: ix
children.text: Innovative methods of promoting achievement of high-quality employment outcomes.

children.label: x
children.text: The demonstration of the effectiveness of early intervention activities in improving employment outcomes.

children.label: xi
children.text: Alternative methods of providing affordable transportation services to individuals with disabilities who are employed, seeking employment, or receiving vocational rehabilitation services from public or private organizations and who reside in geographic areas in which public transportation or paratransit service is not available.

children.heading: Parent information and training program
children.label: c Parent information and training program

children.heading: Grants
children.label: 1 Grants
children.text: The Commissioner is authorized to make grants to private nonprofit organizations for the purpose of establishing programs to provide training and information to enable individuals with disabilities, and the parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or other authorized representatives of the individuals to participate more effectively with professionals in meeting the vocational, independent living, and rehabilitation needs of individuals with disabilities. Such grants shall be designed to meet the unique training and information needs of the individuals described in the preceding sentence, who live in the area to be served, particularly those who are members of populations that have been unserved or underserved by programs under this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: to better understand vocational rehabilitation and independent living programs and services;

children.label: B
children.text: to provide followup support for transition and employment programs;

children.label: C
children.text: to communicate more effectively with transition and rehabilitation personnel and other relevant professionals;

children.label: D
children.text: to provide support in the development of the individualized plan for employment;

children.label: E
children.text: to provide support and expertise in obtaining information about rehabilitation and independent living programs, services, and resources that are appropriate;

children.label: F
children.text: to provide support and guidance in helping individuals with significant disabilities, including students with disabilities, transition to competitive integrated employment; and

children.label: G
children.text: to understand the provisions of this chapter, particularly provisions relating to employment, supported employment, and independent living.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be distributed geographically to the greatest extent possible throughout all States; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be targeted to individuals with disabilities, and the parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of the individuals, in both urban and rural areas or on a State or regional basis.

children.label: i
children.text: to coordinate training and information activities with Centers for Independent Living;

children.label: ii
children.text: to coordinate and work closely with the parent training and information centers established pursuant to , the community parent resource centers established pursuant to , and the eligible entities receiving awards under ; andsection 1471 of title 20section 1472 of title 20section 1473 of title 20

children.label: iii
children.text: to effectively conduct the training and information activities authorized under this subsection;

children.label: I
children.text: that includes professionals in the field of vocational rehabilitation; and

children.label: II
children.text: on which a majority of the members are individuals with disabilities or the parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of the individuals; or

children.label: I
children.text: shall have a membership that represents the interests of individuals with disabilities; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall establish a special governing committee that meets the requirements specified in subclauses (I) and (II) of clause (i) to operate a training and information program under this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall serve, and demonstrate the capacity for serving, individuals with a full range of disabilities, and the parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of the individuals.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 5 Consultation
children.text: Each organization carrying out a program receiving assistance under this subsection shall consult with appropriate agencies that serve or assist individuals with disabilities, and the parents, family members, guardians, advocates, or authorized representatives of the individuals, located in the jurisdiction served by the program.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 6 Coordination
children.text: The Commissioner shall provide coordination and technical assistance by grant or cooperative agreement for establishing, developing, and coordinating the training and information programs. To the extent practicable, such assistance shall be provided by the parent training and information centers established pursuant to .section 1471 of title 20

children.heading: Quarterly review
children.label: A Quarterly review
children.text: The board of directors or special governing committee of an organization receiving a grant under this subsection shall meet at least once in each calendar quarter to review the training and information program, and each such committee shall directly advise the governing board regarding the views and recommendations of the committee.

children.heading: Review for grant renewal
children.label: B Review for grant renewal
children.text: If a nonprofit private organization requests the renewal of a grant under this subsection, the board of directors or the special governing committee shall prepare and submit to the Commissioner a written review of the training and information program conducted by the organization during the preceding fiscal year.

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: 8 Reservation
children.text: From the amount appropriated to carry out this section for a fiscal year, 20 percent of such amount or $500,000, whichever is less, may be reserved to carry out paragraph (6).

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Commissioner shall make grants to, and enter into contracts with, States and public or nonprofit agencies and organizations, including institutions of higher education, to pay all or part of the cost of training in the use of braille for personnel providing vocational rehabilitation services or educational services to youth and adults who are blind.

children.label: A
children.text: development of braille training materials;

children.label: B
children.text: in-service or pre-service training in the use of braille, the importance of braille literacy, and methods of teaching braille to youth and adults who are blind; and

children.label: C
children.text: activities to promote knowledge and use of braille and nonvisual access technology for blind youth and adults through a program of training, demonstration, and evaluation conducted with leadership of experienced blind individuals, including the use of comprehensive, state-of-the-art technology.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 3 Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant, or enter into a contract, under paragraph (1), an agency or organization shall submit an application to the Commissioner at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Commissioner may require.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this section there are authorized to be appropriated $5,796,000 for fiscal year 2015, $6,244,000 for fiscal year 2016, $6,373,000 for fiscal year 2017, $6,515,000 for fiscal year 2018, $6,668,000 for fiscal year 2019, and $6,809,000 for fiscal year 2020.
